description: PeruShopHub UI/UX Design System & Application Layout - Complete Angular frontend with design tokens, application shell, and all MVP screens

userStories.title: Scaffold Angular project with routing and module structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Angular 17+ project scaffolded with proper module structure, routing configuration, and base dependencies so all subsequent UI work has a foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Angular 17+ project created at src/PeruShopHub.Web/ using standalone components
Install dependencies: ng2-charts, chart.js, lucide-angular (icons)
App routing configured with lazy-loaded routes for: dashboard, produtos, vendas, perguntas, clientes, financeiro, estoque, configuracoes, login
Google Fonts loaded: Inter (400,500,600,700) and Roboto Mono (400,500)
Base index.html with proper lang='pt-BR' and meta viewport for responsive
Proxy config for API calls to backend (proxy.conf.json targeting localhost:5000)
ng serve runs without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation. No UI components yet, just project structure and routing stubs.

userStories.title: Design tokens CSS custom properties with light and dark themes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a CSS custom properties system with all design tokens (colors, typography, spacing, elevation, breakpoints) for both light and dark themes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create styles/tokens.css with all CSS custom properties from the design system: colors (primary, accent, success, warning, danger, neutrals, surface, page-bg), typography sizes, spacing scale, elevation (shadows), border radii
Light theme tokens in :root selector
Dark theme tokens in :root[data-theme='dark'] selector
Breakpoint variables as CSS custom properties
SCSS mixins file (styles/mixins.scss) with responsive breakpoint mixins: @mixin mobile, @mixin tablet, @mixin desktop, @mixin desktop-lg
styles.scss imports tokens.css and sets base body styles (font-family Inter, background var(--page-bg), color var(--neutral-900))
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Colors: primary #1A237E, accent #FF6F00. See Docs/PeruShopHub-Design-System.md for full token list.

userStories.title: Theme service with toggle, persistence, and system preference detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ThemeService that manages light/dark theme switching, persists preference to localStorage, and respects prefers-color-scheme on first visit.
acceptanceCriteria: ThemeService as injectable root service with currentTheme signal: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'
On init: check localStorage('theme-preference'), fallback to 'system'
When 'system': use window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)') and listen for changes
setTheme(theme) method updates data-theme attribute on document.documentElement and persists to localStorage
toggleTheme() method cycles light → dark → system
Theme switches instantly without page reload
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared pipes: BRL currency formatting and relative date
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable pipes for BRL currency formatting (R$ 1.234,56) and relative date display (ha 2h, ha 1d).
acceptanceCriteria: BrlCurrencyPipe: transforms number to 'R$ 1.234,56' format using pt-BR locale. Supports optional 'compact' mode for 'R$ 18,4k' / 'R$ 1,2M'
RelativeDatePipe: transforms Date/string to relative format for recent dates ('ha 2h', 'ha 1d', 'ha 3sem') and absolute for older ('15 mar 2026, 14:30'). Threshold: 7 days
Both pipes are standalone and exported from shared module
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared components: Badge and KPI Card
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable Badge and KPI Card components that follow the design system.
acceptanceCriteria: BadgeComponent: standalone, inputs: label (string), variant ('success'|'warning'|'danger'|'primary'|'neutral'|'accent'). Renders pill-shaped badge with correct background/text colors per variant from design tokens

KpiCardComponent: standalone, inputs: label (string), value (string), change (number, optional), changeLabel (string, optional), invertColors (boolean, default false for revenue-like metrics, true for cost-like). Renders: label in body-small neutral-700, value in metric-large, change arrow+percentage colored green/red based on positive/negative and invertColors flag
Both components responsive (KPI card uses metric-medium on mobile)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared components: Empty state, Error state, and Loading skeleton
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable empty state, error state, and skeleton loading components for consistent UX across all screens.
acceptanceCriteria: EmptyStateComponent: standalone, inputs: title (string), description (string), icon (string, optional), actionLabel (string, optional), actionEmitter (output event). Renders centered block with icon area, title, description, optional CTA button
ErrorStateComponent: standalone, inputs: title (default 'Algo deu errado'), description (string), retryEmitter (output). Renders error icon, title, description, 'Tentar novamente' button
SkeletonComponent: standalone, inputs: type ('text'|'rect'|'circle'), width (string), height (string). Renders shimmer-animated placeholder shape
All components use design tokens for colors and spacing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared component: Responsive data table with mobile card transformation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a wrapper component that renders a data table on desktop/tablet and transforms to a card list on mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: DataTableComponent: standalone, inputs: columns (array of {key, label, align, format, sortable}), data (array), loading (boolean), emptyTitle (string), emptyDescription (string), pageSize (number), totalItems (number)
Desktop (>=1024px): renders standard HTML table with sortable column headers (click to toggle asc/desc), cell alignment per column config, pagination bar at bottom
Tablet (768-1023px): table with horizontal scroll, first column sticky
Mobile (<768px): each row renders as a card with label:value pairs stacked vertically
Loading state: shows 6 skeleton rows
Empty state: shows EmptyStateComponent
Pagination bar: 'Mostrando X-Y de Z', page size selector (10/25/50), prev/next buttons
Sort and page change emit output events for server-side handling
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared component: Toast notification service and component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a toast notification system for success/error/warning feedback messages.
acceptanceCriteria: ToastService: injectable root service with show(message, type, duration?) method. Types: 'success'|'warning'|'danger'|'info'
ToastContainerComponent: renders in app root, positioned top-right with 24px margin
Each toast: colored left border per type, icon, title, optional description, X close button
Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds (configurable), max 3 visible, stack with 8px gap
Slide-in animation from right (200ms)
On mobile: full width minus 24px padding
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Application shell: Collapsible sidebar navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a collapsible sidebar so I can navigate between sections while preserving screen space for data.
acceptanceCriteria: SidebarComponent: standalone, renders vertical navigation with 8 items: Dashboard, Produtos, Vendas, Perguntas, Clientes, Financeiro, Estoque, Configuracoes
Each item has Lucide icon + label text
PeruShopHub logo at top (text when expanded, icon-only when collapsed)
Collapse toggle button at bottom toggles between 256px (expanded) and 64px (collapsed, icons only)
Active route highlighted with --primary-light background and --primary text
Hover state: --neutral-100 background
Collapse state persisted to localStorage via SidebarService
Smooth width transition (200ms ease)
On mobile (<768px): sidebar is an overlay drawer with --overlay backdrop, triggered externally
On tablet (768-1023px): starts collapsed
On desktop (1024px+): starts expanded
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Application shell: Header bar with user menu and theme toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want a top header bar with sidebar toggle, placeholder search, theme toggle, and user dropdown.
acceptanceCriteria: HeaderComponent: standalone, fixed at top, 56px height, --surface background, bottom border
Left: hamburger icon button that emits toggle event (for sidebar)
Center: search input with magnifying glass icon, placeholder 'Buscar pedidos, produtos, clientes...' (non-functional placeholder for now)
Right: marketplace connection dot (green/red, hardcoded green for now)
Right: notification bell icon with hardcoded badge count (non-functional placeholder)
Right: theme toggle button (sun/moon icon) that calls ThemeService.toggleTheme()
Right: user avatar circle with dropdown menu on click: name, email, role, separator, 'Configuracoes' link, 'Sair' link. Dropdown closes on outside click and Esc
Responsive: on mobile, hide search input, show only hamburger + bell + avatar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Application shell: Main layout combining sidebar, header, and router outlet
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the main layout component that combines sidebar, header, and content area with proper responsive behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: LayoutComponent: standalone, renders HeaderComponent at top (fixed), SidebarComponent on left, and <router-outlet> in main content area
Content area has left margin matching sidebar width (256px expanded, 64px collapsed), top margin 56px for header
Content area scrolls independently (overflow-y auto)
Content area has page padding: 24px desktop, 16px tablet, 12px mobile
On mobile: sidebar margin is 0 (sidebar is overlay)
Sidebar toggle in header opens/closes sidebar via shared SidebarService
Login route renders WITHOUT LayoutComponent (full-page login)
All other routes render inside LayoutComponent
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Login screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want a login screen to authenticate into the system.
acceptanceCriteria: LoginComponent: standalone, rendered at /login route WITHOUT the app shell layout
Centered card (max-width 400px) with PeruShopHub logo + tagline 'Gestao inteligente de marketplaces'
Reactive form with email field (required, email validator) and password field (required, min 6 chars)
Labels above fields, inline error messages below on blur/submit
'Entrar' primary button full width, disabled when form invalid
'Esqueceu a senha?' link below button (navigates nowhere for now)
Loading state: button shows spinner, fields disabled (simulated with 1.5s timeout for now)
Error state: 'Credenciais invalidas' alert below form
Background: subtle gradient from --primary to --primary-hover
Responsive: card 100% width with 16px padding on mobile, max 400px centered on desktop
Supports dark theme
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: No real auth backend yet. Simulate login with hardcoded credentials or just accept any input.

userStories.title: Dashboard: Period selector and KPI cards row
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see key performance indicators on the dashboard with a period selector.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardComponent at /dashboard route
Period selector at top: button group with 'Hoje', '7 dias', '30 dias', 'Personalizado'. Active button styled with --primary. 'Personalizado' shows date range inputs on click
4 KPI cards in responsive grid (4 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile) using KpiCardComponent
KPIs: Vendas (127, +15.3%), Receita Bruta (R$ 18.432,00, +8.7%), Lucro Liquido (R$ 4.210,50, -2.1%), Margem Media (22.8%, -1.3pp)
Data is hardcoded/mock for now
Loading state: 4 skeleton cards
Period change updates displayed data (can be same mock data with different label)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Mock data only. Real API integration comes later.

userStories.title: Dashboard: Sales and profit line chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a line chart showing revenue and profit trends over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Chart.js line chart to dashboard below KPI cards, taking 60% width on desktop (left side)
Title: 'Vendas e Lucro'
X-axis: dates (last 30 days, daily)
Two lines: 'Receita Bruta' (--primary color, solid) and 'Lucro Liquido' (--success color, dashed)
Subtle area fill below each line (opacity 0.1)
Tooltip on hover: date + Receita: R$ X + Lucro: R$ Y
Clickable legend above chart to toggle lines
Mock data: 30 data points with realistic-looking random values
Responsive: full width on tablet/mobile, stacks below KPIs
Chart uses design token colors (respects dark theme)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard: Cost distribution donut chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want a donut chart showing how my costs are distributed across categories.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart.js doughnut chart on dashboard, 40% width on desktop (right of line chart)
Title: 'Distribuicao de Custos'
Segments: Comissao ML (#5C6BC0), Frete (#42A5F5), Custo Produto (#66BB6A), Embalagem (#FFA726), Impostos (#EF5350), Armazenagem (#AB47BC), Advertising (#26C6DA), Outros (#BDBDBD)
Center text: 'Total Custos' + R$ value
Tooltip: category name + value + percentage
Legend below chart in 2 columns
Mock data with realistic proportions
Responsive: full width on mobile, stacks below line chart
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard: Top products tables and pending actions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my most/least profitable products and pending actions on the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Two side-by-side tables below charts (stack on mobile): 'Top 5 Mais Lucrativos' and 'Top 5 Menos Lucrativos'
Columns: Produto, Vendas, Receita (BRL), Lucro (BRL), Margem %
Margin column colored: green >=20%, yellow 10-19%, red <10%
Rows with negative margin highlighted with --danger-light background
Rows clickable (console.log product ID for now, navigation later)
Below tables: 'Acoes Pendentes' card with 3 clickable badges: 'X Perguntas sem resposta', 'Y Pedidos pendentes', 'Z Alertas'. Badges use accent/warning/danger colors
All data is mock/hardcoded
Empty state for dashboard when no data: centered illustration text 'Conecte seu Mercado Livre para comecar' with 'Conectar Marketplace' button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Products list screen with table, filters, and pagination
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my products in a filterable, sortable table.
acceptanceCriteria: ProductsListComponent at /produtos route
Page title 'Produtos' with 'Novo Produto' accent button (navigates to /produtos/novo)
Action bar: search input (by name/SKU), status filter dropdown (Todos/Ativo/Pausado/Encerrado)
Uses DataTableComponent with columns: Foto (40x40 thumbnail), Nome, SKU (mono font), Preco (BRL), Estoque, Status (BadgeComponent), Margem (% colored), Acoes (edit icon)
10 rows of mock product data with realistic Brazilian product names
Row click navigates to /produtos/:id
Empty state: 'Nenhum produto cadastrado' with 'Novo Produto' CTA
Loading skeleton state
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Product create/edit form: Basic info, pricing, and shipping tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create and edit products with organized form sections.
acceptanceCriteria: ProductFormComponent at /produtos/novo and /produtos/:id/editar routes
Page title 'Novo Produto' or 'Editar Produto: {name}'
Tab navigation with 3 tabs: 'Informacoes Basicas', 'Preco e Custos', 'Envio'
Informacoes Basicas tab: titulo input (max 60 chars with character counter), descricao textarea, categoria searchable dropdown (hardcoded list), condicao radio (Novo/Usado)

Preco e Custos tab: preco de venda (R$ prefix input), custo de aquisicao, custo embalagem, fornecedor text input, tipo de anuncio select (Gratis/Classico/Premium). Real-time margin calculator: 'Margem estimada: X%' calculated as (preco - custo - comissao_estimada) / preco * 100
Envio tab: dimensoes inputs (peso kg, altura cm, largura cm, comprimento cm), frete gratis toggle
All fields use Angular Reactive Forms with validation
Sticky bottom bar: 'Cancelar' ghost button, 'Salvar Rascunho' outline button, 'Publicar' accent button
Unsaved changes guard: warn on navigation if form is dirty
Responsive: tabs become accordion on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Photos, attributes, and variations tabs deferred to separate story.

userStories.title: Product detail screen

Header section: product photo (placeholder), name, SKU (mono), status badge, 'Editar' outline button
Metrics row using KpiCardComponent: Vendas 30d, Receita 30d, Lucro 30d, Margem 30d, Estoque
Section 'Historico de Custos': simple table showing date, cost type, value with 3-5 mock rows
Section 'Vendas Recentes': mini table with last 5 mock orders (ID, date, qty, value, profit)
All data is mock/hardcoded
Back button to /produtos list

userStories.title: Order detail: Header, items, buyer, shipping, and payment sections
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see order information including items, buyer, shipping, and payment details.
acceptanceCriteria: OrderDetailComponent at /vendas/:id route
Header: 'Pedido #2087654321', date, status badge, back button to /vendas
Two-column layout on desktop (stacked on mobile)
Left column - Section 'Itens do Pedido': product thumbnail, name, SKU, variation, quantity, unit price, subtotal
Right column - Section 'Comprador': name, nickname, email (masked), phone (masked), total orders, total spent

Right column - Section 'Envio': tracking number with copy button, carrier name, logistic type badge (Full/Coleta/Agencia), delivery timeline (4 steps: criado → pago → enviado → entregue with dates, completed steps filled, future steps empty circle)
Right column - Section 'Pagamento': payment method icon + name, installments, amount, status badge
All mock data

userStories.title: Order detail: Cost breakdown stacked bar and table (CRITICAL)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the full cost breakdown for an order with a visual stacked bar and detailed table so I understand exactly how much I earned.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Decomposicao de Custos' section to OrderDetailComponent (left column, below items)

Stacked horizontal bar (32px height, full width): segments proportional to each cost as % of revenue. Each segment colored per cost category. Final segment = lucro in --success color. Tooltip on hover showing category name + value + %
Cost breakdown table below bar with columns: Categoria (text), Valor (BRL, mono, right-aligned), % Receita (mono, right-aligned), Fonte (badge: API=primary, Manual=warning, Calculado=success)
Rows: Comissao ML, Taxa fixa, Frete vendedor, Taxa de pagamento, Custo do produto, Embalagem, Impostos, Armazenagem, Advertising
Separator line, then TOTAL CUSTOS row (bold, --neutral-100 background)
Double-border separator, then LUCRO LIQUIDO row (metric-medium font, colored green if positive / red if negative)
Margin percentage displayed next to lucro value
'+ Adicionar custo manual' text button below table (non-functional for now)
Mock data matching the example from the design spec (R$ 159.90 revenue)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the product's core differentiator screen. Must be pixel-perfect per Docs/PeruShopHub-Design-System.md section 4.

userStories.title: Questions screen with inline response

acceptanceCriteria: QuestionsComponent at /perguntas route
Page title 'Perguntas'
Tab filters: 'Sem Resposta' (with count badge), 'Respondidas', 'Todas'
Each question rendered as a card: product thumbnail + title, buyer nickname, question text, relative timestamp
Time indicator turns --danger color if > 24h unanswered
Unanswered cards have 'Responder' accent outline button
Clicking 'Responder' expands textarea below question in same card, with 'Enviar' button and 'Cancelar' link
On submit: card moves to 'Respondidas' tab, success toast shown
Unanswered sorted oldest first
8 mock questions (5 unanswered, 3 answered)
Responsive: full-width cards on mobile
Typecheck passes

Sortable by Total Gasto, Total Pedidos, Ultima Compra
Row click opens slide-over panel (400px from right, overlay backdrop): buyer info at top, order history table below (5 mock orders: ID, date, value, status badge)
Slide-over closes on backdrop click, Esc key, or X button

userStories.title: Global search command palette (Ctrl+K)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search across orders, products, and customers via a keyboard-triggered command palette.
acceptanceCriteria: SearchPaletteComponent: modal overlay triggered by Ctrl+K / Cmd+K or clicking header search input
Input at top with search icon, auto-focused on open
Debounced search (300ms) filters mock data
Results grouped by type: 'Pedidos' (order icon), 'Produtos' (package icon), 'Clientes' (users icon) — max 3 per group
Each result: type icon, primary text (name/ID), secondary text (SKU, date, email)
Arrow keys navigate results (highlighted row), Enter navigates to detail screen, Esc closes
Click on result navigates to detail screen
No results state: 'Nenhum resultado para "{query}"'
Mock dataset: 10 products, 15 orders, 10 customers searchable by name/ID/SKU

userStories.title: 404 page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a friendly 404 page when I navigate to an unknown route.
acceptanceCriteria: NotFoundComponent at wildcard route **

Centered content: large '404' text (metric-large), title 'Pagina nao encontrada', description 'A pagina que voce procura nao existe ou foi movida.', 'Voltar ao Dashboard' primary button linking to /dashboard
Rendered without app shell (full page) or within shell — either is acceptable
Supports dark theme
